name: woocommerce-copy-guidelines
description: Guidelines for UI text and copy in WooCommerce. Use when writing user-facing text, labels, buttons, or messages.
capabilities: Apply sentence case to UI labels, buttons, headings, and messages
Format acronyms correctly in user-facing text
Ensure consistency with existing WooCommerce patterns
author: woocommerce
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidelines for UI text. All 23 static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified markdown code fences as shell backticks, plain text examples as system reconnaissance, and metadata fields as cryptographic issues. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or command execution exists.
valueStatement: Inconsistent capitalization in UI text creates confusion and reduces accessibility. This skill provides clear rules for sentence case usage in WooCommerce copy, ensuring user-facing text is readable, modern, and accessible across all languages.
userTitle: Apply consistent sentence case to UI text
limitations: Does not validate text against accessibility guidelines beyond capitalization
Does not translate or localize text
Does not generate new copy content

useCases.title: Write consistent WooCommerce UI
useCases.description: Ensure plugin UI text follows WooCommerce sentence case standards for consistent user experience.

useCases.title: Review UI text formatting
useCases.description: Verify that documentation and user-facing copy uses proper sentence case throughout.

useCases.title: Guide copy generation
useCases.description: Apply sentence case rules when generating buttons, labels, messages, and interface text.

promptTemplates.title: Format button text
promptTemplates.prompt: Write sentence case button labels for these actions: save settings, add product, continue checkout, send test email

promptTemplates.title: Format labels
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert these labels to sentence case: Payment Method, Shipping Address, Order Notes, Tax Rate

promptTemplates.title: Handle brand names
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a message mentioning WooPayments, Stripe, PayPal, and WordPress in sentence case format

promptTemplates.title: Review notification
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this title case notification to sentence case: Settings Saved Successfully, Unable To Connect To Payment Provider

bestPractices: Capitalize only the first word and proper nouns in UI text
Keep sentences short, action-oriented, and conversational
Use consistent patterns across similar UI elements
antiPatterns: Using title case for all words in labels and buttons
Capitalizing every word in error messages and notifications
Ignoring proper noun exceptions for brand names

faq.question: Does this skill work with all AI assistants?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidelines compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for writing consistent UI copy.

faq.question: What is the main rule for sentence case?
faq.answer: Capitalize only the first word, proper nouns, and acronyms. All other words use lowercase.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-WooCommerce projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The sentence case principles apply to any UI text regardless of the platform or framework.

faq.question: Does this skill access any user data?
faq.answer: No. This skill contains only static guidelines and does not read, write, or transmit any data.

faq.question: What exceptions exist for sentence case?
faq.answer: Always capitalize proper nouns, acronyms, and official brand names like PayPal, Stripe, and WooCommerce.

faq.question: How is this different from title case?
faq.answer: Sentence case capitalizes only the first word while title case capitalizes most words. Sentence case is more accessible.
